A FARM BUILDING HAD SEVERAL METAL PANELS BLOWN OUT AND LOFTED TO THE NORTHEAST.

A 90 MPH MICROBURST WAS DETERMINED TO HAVE HIT ROACH`S FARM IN VENICE, NY. THERE WERE FOUR BARNS THAT SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE WITH METAL PANELS BEING LOFTED. SOME OF THE METAL PANELS HAD BEEN LOFTED INTO THE POWER LINES ALONG STEWART`S CORNERS RD BUT HAD BEEN CLEANED PRIOR TO OUR SURVEY. THERE WERE SEVERAL PIECES OF METAL ROOFING THAT WERE STILL LOCATED IN TREES AT THE TIME OF OUR SURVEY. THE STRONGEST DAMAGE INDICATORS WERE ON THE NORTHWESTERN EDGE OF THE DAMAGE PATH ALONG FORD RD, WHERE SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED. THREE OF THE HEMLOCK TREES LINING FORD ROAD WERE SNAPPED ROUGHLY 10 TO 15 FEET OFF THE GROUND. BEHIND THOSE TREES, THERE WERE A GROUP OF MAPLE TREES WITH SEVERAL OF THOSE BEING SNAPPED IN ADDITION TO THE HEMLOCKS. ONE OF THE MAPLE TREES FELL ONTO A FARM BUILDING AND SHIFTED THE ENTIRE BUILDING OFF THE WOODEN FOUNDATION TO THE EAST NORTHEAST. THE NWS IN BINGHAMTON WOULD LIKE TO THANK THE TOWN OF VENICE HIGHWAY DEPARTMENT FOR THEIR ASSISTANCE IN LOCATING THE DAMAGE AND HELPING DURING THE SURVEY.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS EF1 TORNADO FROM 1.5 WNW WAYLAND TO 0.5 SE WAYLAND, 733-738 PM EDT MONDAY 09/19/2022. A FEW TRUNK SNAPS, SOME UPROOTED TREES, NUMEROUS DOWNED LARGE BRANCHES IN A CONVERGENT DEFINED 1.9 MILE PATH, MAX WIDTH 150 YARDS. SOME PROPERTY DAMAGE TO VEHICLES, ROOFS, FENCES, AND A POOL. CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM WAYLAND. TREES AND WIRES DOWN. SOME VILLAGE RESIDENTS WITHOUT POWER.

CORRECTS THE TIME FROM THE PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 1 NNW BEERSTON. TREES AND WIRES DOWN NEAR INTERSECTION OF WALTON MOUNTAIN RD AND STATE ROUTE 10.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE IN BINGHAMTON NY CONFIRMED WITH A STORM SURVEY, THAT A TORNADO MOVED THROUGH THE WALTON AREA ON THE EVENING OF SEPTEMBER 25TH BETWEEN 6:38 PM TO 6:41 PM. DURING THE SURVEY, TORNADO DAMAGE WAS FIRST NOTED ALONG WALTON MOUNTAIN RD WHERE TREES FELL IN A CONVERGENT PATTERN. TALKING TO SEVERAL RESIDENTS, WINDS CAME FROM MULTIPLE DIRECTIONS, WITH LAWN FURNITURE AND PART OF A DECK RAILING BLOWN NORTHWEST WHILE TREES IN THE BACK YARD WERE BLOWN DOWN FACING SOUTHWEST. SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE WAS FOLLOWED DOWN TO ROUTE 10 TO AN AUTOSHOP WITH SOME METAL SHEETING RIPPED OFF OF THE ROOF AND THROWN ABOUT A 100 YARDS. FROM THERE THE DAMAGE PICKED BACK UP ACROSS THE WEST BRANCH OF THE DELAWARE RIVER ALONG S RIVER RD. SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE CONTINUED ALONG THE SOUTH SHORE OF THE RIVER TO JUST SOUTH OF S RIVER RD WITH THE WORST TREE DAMAGE OCCURRING NEAR THE END OF THE TORNADO`S TRACK. UP UNTIL THIS SECTION, THE DAMAGE FROM THE TORNADO WAS EF0. MANY LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AND FELL IN A CROSS STITCH PATTERN FOR A SECTION OF FOREST THAT WAS ABOUT 300 YARDS LONG AND 50 YARDS WIDE WHERE THE EF1 DAMAGE WAS FOUND.
WIRES DOWN. TRANSFORMER FIRE.
